fault_hypothesis: The overlap detection on line 67 is incorrect. It uses non-strict inequalities (`<=`, `>=`), which fails to detect conflicts when intervals touch at their boundaries, causing them to be incorrectly accepted.

spec.expr: (end <= s or start >= e) == (end < s or start > e)

constraints.intent: The non-overlap condition should use strict inequalities to correctly handle boundary cases where intervals touch.
If an iteration completes without finding a conflict, the new interval must be strictly non-overlapping with the current existing interval.

spec.expr: all(interval[1] < s or interval[0] > e for s, e in existing)

constraints.intent: If the loop finishes without returning, it implies the new interval does not conflict with any existing one.

spec.expr: not __cbfl_result[0] or all(__cbfl_result[1][i][1] < __cbfl_result[1][i+1][0] for i in range(len(__cbfl_result[1]) - 1))

constraints.intent: If an interval is successfully added, the resulting schedule must not contain any overlapping or touching intervals.
